statement: The historical partial D1 run was correctly accounted as three exhausted rubric-generation targets that blocked exactly thirty downstream score units, not a hidden loss of score-stage work.
rationale: Three samples have no rubric artifacts, each of those samples has exactly ten missing score units, and downstream score targets and requests are absent because the units were blocked upstream.
keyAssumptions: The sampled failed samples are representative of the full failed set.
No additional missing units exist outside the three failed rubric samples.
validationCriteria: Exactly three samples are fully missing downstream score artifacts.
Targeted failed samples show exhausted rubric targets and no score-stage target or request for missing units.
critiquePoints: Diagnostics show multiple failed rubric attempts, so attempts and targets must not be conflated.
This does not prove the rubric parser bug itself is fixed for future runs.
questions: Do other historical partial runs show the same blocked-downstream pattern?
